Record Class Right<L,R>

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Record
io.github.igorgf.control.Right<L,R>
Type Parameters:
L - The left value type.
R - The contained value type.
Record Components:
value - The contained value R. Never null.
All Implemented Interfaces:
Either<L,R>
record Right<L,R>(R value) extends Record implements Either<L,R>
The right R value implementation of Either<L, R>.

Contains a NEVER null value R.

See Also:
  • Either
  • Left

  • Constructor Details

    • Right

      Right(R value)
      Creates an instance of a Right record class.
      Parameters:

  • Method Details

    • bimap

      public <S, T, X1 extends Exception, X2 extends Exception> Either<S,T> bimap(CheckedFunction<? super L, ? extends S, ? extends X1> leftMapper, CheckedFunction<? super R, ? extends T, ? extends X2> rightMapper) throws X2
      The bi-functor operation of Either. Transforms the right value using the provided rightMapper function, or the left value using the provided leftMapper function.

      When this is a Right then the rightMapper is applied to the contained value, and contained within a new Right<S, T>. When this is a Left then the leftMapper is applied to the contained value, and contained within a new Left<S, T>, allowing for convenient chaining.

      The rightMapper and leftMapper don't have to be concerned with handling null contained values as value() or Left.value() can never be null.

      Checked exceptions thrown by rightMapper or leftMapper are propagated, see Either class documentation for details.

      Specified by:
      bimap in interface Either<L,R>
      Type Parameters:
      S - The Left type being contained after mapping.
      T - The Right type being contained after mapping.
      Parameters:
      leftMapper - The mapping function to apply to the contained value, if this is a Left.
      rightMapper - The mapping function to apply to the contained value, if this is a Right.
      Returns:
      Right<S, T> with the value T returned by the rightMapper
      Throws:
      X2 - If the rightMapper throws a checked exception, it is propagated to the caller.
      NullPointerException - If the rightMapper is null or returns a null.
      See Also:
      • bimap(CheckedFunction, CheckedFunction)
      • Left.bimap(CheckedFunction, CheckedFunction)

    • biflatMap

      public <S, T, X1 extends Exception, X2 extends Exception> Either<S,T> biflatMap(CheckedFunction<? super L, ? extends Either<S,T>, ? extends X1> leftMapper, CheckedFunction<? super R, ? extends Either<S,T>, ? extends X2> rightMapper) throws X2
      The bi-functor monadic chaining operation of Either. Transforms the right value using the provided rightMapper function, or the left value using the provided leftMapper function.

      This method is similar to Either.bimap(CheckedFunction, CheckedFunction), but the mapping functions are ones whose result is already an ? extends Either<S, T>, and if invoked, biflatMap does not wrap it within an additional Either.

      If either mapping returns a null then this method throws a NullPointerException. Each mapper must always return a ? extends Either<S, T>.

      The rightMapper and leftMapper don't have to be concerned with handling null contained values as value() or Left.value() can never be null.

      Checked exceptions thrown by rightMapper or leftMapper are propagated, see Either class documentation for details.

      Specified by:
      biflatMap in interface Either<L,R>
      Type Parameters:
      S - The Left type being contained after mapping.
      T - The Right type being contained after mapping.
      Parameters:
      leftMapper - The mapping function to apply to the contained value, if this is a Left.
      rightMapper - The mapping function to apply to the contained value, if this is a Right.
      Returns:
      Either<S, T> returned by the rightMapper.
      Throws:
      X2 - If the rightMapper throws a checked exception, it is propagated to the caller.
      NullPointerException - If the rightMapper is null or returns a null.
      See Also:
      • Either.bimap(CheckedFunction, CheckedFunction)
      • biflatMap(CheckedFunction, CheckedFunction)
      • Left.biflatMap(CheckedFunction, CheckedFunction)

    • swap

      public Either<R,L> swap()
      Converts this Right into a Left<R, L> containing value().
      Specified by:
      swap in interface Either<L,R>
      Returns:
      A new Left<R, L> containing value().

    • fold

      public <T, X1 extends Exception, X2 extends Exception> T fold(CheckedFunction<? super L, ? extends T, ? extends X1> leftMapper, CheckedFunction<? super R, ? extends T, ? extends X2> rightMapper) throws X2
      The catamorphism operation of Either. Collapses both possible states (Left and Right) into a single value.

      This method is similar to Either.bimap(CheckedFunction, CheckedFunction), but forgoes the re-containing of the mapping results, and returns a concrete value T instead.

      The rightMapper and leftMapper don't have to be concerned with handling null contained values as value() or Left.value() can never be null.

      Checked exceptions thrown by rightMapper or leftMapper are propagated, see Either class documentation for details.

      Specified by:
      fold in interface Either<L,R>
      Type Parameters:
      T - The result type after mapping.
      Parameters:
      leftMapper - The mapping function to apply to the contained value, if this is a Left.
      rightMapper - The mapping function to apply to the contained value, if this is a Right.
      Returns:
      T returned by the rightMapper.
      Throws:
      X2 - If the rightMapper throws a checked exception, it is propagated to the caller.
      NullPointerException - If the rightMapper is null or returns a null.
      See Also:
      • Either.bimap(CheckedFunction, CheckedFunction)
      • Present.fold(CheckedFunction, CheckedSupplier)
      • Empty.fold(CheckedFunction, CheckedSupplier)

    • isLeft

      Is this a Left.

      isLeft in interface Either<L,R>
      Returns:

      See Also:

    • isRight

      public boolean isRight()
      Is this a Right.
      Specified by:
      isRight in interface Either<L,R>
      Returns:
      right
      See Also:
      • Either.isLeft()

    • getLeft

      public Option<L> getLeft()
      Projects this into an instance of Option.

      When this is a Left, the result is a new Present representation of Option, preserving the contained value and the type L. Otherwise, returns a new Empty only preserving the type constraint L.

      This is a more terse and intention revealing equivalent to doing a Either.fold(CheckedFunction, CheckedFunction) into an Option: 

          Either<Integer, String> original = Either.left(8);
    Option<Integer> folded = original.fold(Option::of, _ -> Option.empty());
    assert original.getLeft().equals(folded);
      Specified by:
      getLeft in interface Either<L,R>
      Returns:
      A new Empty<L>.
      See Also:
      • Empty

    • getRight

      public Option<R> getRight()
      Projects this into an instance of Option.

      When this is a Right, the result is a new Present representation of Option, preserving the contained value and the type R. Otherwise, returns a new Empty only preserving the type constraint R.

      This is a more terse and intention revealing equivalent to doing a Either.fold(CheckedFunction, CheckedFunction) into an Option: 

          Either<String, Integer> original = Either.right(8);
    Option<Integer> folded = original.fold(_ -> Option.empty(), Option::of);
    assert original.getRight().equals(folded);
      Specified by:
      getRight in interface Either<L,R>
      Returns:
      A new Present<R>.
      See Also:
      • Present

    • toString

      public final String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this record class. The representation contains the name of the class, followed by the name and value of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      toString in class Record
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this object. The value is derived from the hash code of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      hashCode in class Record
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object o)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. The objects are equal if the other object is of the same class and if all the record components are equal. All components in this record class are compared with Objects::equals(Object,Object).
      Specified by:
      equals in class Record
      Parameters:
      o - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the o argument; false otherwise.

    • value

      public R value()
      Returns the value of the value record component.
      Returns:
      the value of the value record component
